ops.title: Locked S3 rename decisions ‚Äî cc-pushback becomes cc-steer everywhere, full publish
ops.body:
The pushback‚Üísteering reframe's S3 (full rename) decisions, locked by the user 2026-07-04 via AskUserQuestion:

- **New name is `cc-steer`** (NOT `cc-steering`) ‚Äî PyPI package `cc-steer`, CLI `cc-steer`, module dir `cc_pushback/` ‚Üí `cc_steer/`, internal `PUSHBACK_*` ‚Üí `STEER*`/`STEERING_*` (STEERING_CATEGORIES already exists), `Verdict.is_pushback` ‚Üí `is_steering`.
- **Migrate the local data dir** `~/.cc-pushback/` ‚Üí `~/.cc-steer/` ‚Äî back up `feedback.db` (4,134 judged rows + refinements) first, `cp` then move, repoint every cli/store default.
- **HF dataset**: create `<user>/cc-steer-traces`, push the current corpus, then **delete the old `cc-pushback-traces`** repo.
- **Full publish autonomously** ‚Äî no stop-before-publish; go through PyPI tag/publish + HF create + GitHub release.
- **No backwards-compat anywhere** (verbatim user constraint): DB renames are one-off manual SQL migrations; code uses new names with zero dual-read/shim/fallback.

Sequencing: S2 (refiner+export reword, non-breaking) lands first, then S3. See [[cc-pushback-two-stage-pipeline]] and the handoff (cc-notes 607cb1e). DB column rename `is_pushback`‚Üí`is_steering` and `refinement.complaint*`‚Üí`direction*` happen in S3's migration.
